text: Plumb must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name 'plumb-dev'
The CLI command must be 'plumb'
Tests must support comment-based markers using '# plumb:req-XXXXXXXX' format
Tests must support function name-based linking using 'test_req_XXXXXXXX_' format
Both linking formats must be supported for backwards compatibility
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction in both conversation analysis and diff analysis methods
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions effectively
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False
The deduplication function must include fallback handling for truncated or failed LLM responses by returning all candidates when the LLM returns None or produces truncated output
Plumb must integrate with git hooks to automatically trigger workflows based on repository events
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs
The programs module must include an extract_outline function that extracts markdown headers from content
The programs module must include token estimation, chunking, and concurrent mapper functionality to handle large datasets
The system must use chunked mapping with ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent processing
The system must support different merge strategies for result combination
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty requirements to the mapper for processing
The hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis
If API authentication fails, the hook must exit non-zero and block the commit
The hook must analyze the staged diff and the Claude Code conversation log using the unified conversation reading system
The system must auto-detect Claude Code sessions vs legacy logs
The system must read from Claude Code's actual session files at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/<uuid>.jsonl
The system must write pending decisions to branch-specific decision log files with filesystem-safe path sanitization
The system must set the last_extracted_at timestamp
The hook must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero, aborting the commit
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf
Users must be able to approve multiple pending decisions efficiently using the --all flag with the approve command
Decision operations must support branch-specific handling through branch parameter support
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions
The system must draft commit messages after decision review and include the list of approved decisions
When API authentication fails, the system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception
The PlumbAuthError must alert the user and provide clear instructions to set their API key via environment variable export or in a .env file
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function that is called before all LLM operations
All authentication functionality must have comprehensive test coverage
Existing tests must be updated to mock API validation calls to ensure test suites remain unaffected
The system must support environment variable management using .env files with python-dotenv dependency
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality
Default patterns must be used when no .plumbignore file exists
After commit completion, the post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp to reset the filter for future extractions
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format
The conversation parser must convert tool usage blocks to text format '[tool: Name] description' for consistency
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering, then applying LLM deduplication as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain
The system must use DSPy's context manager with Haiku LM for deduplication while preserving Sonnet for other operations
The deduplication process must prioritize approved and synced decisions over recent ones in the comparison set
The system must use an expanded context window of 200 decisions instead of 50 for more comprehensive duplicate detection
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code
The test generator must produce fully functional test code that can be executed immediately
The test generator must only run if tests don't already exist
The system must provide read_all_decisions() as the primary API function for accessing decisions across all branches
The review command must pre-compute decision branches to optimize performance and ensure correct shard targeting for updates
Users must be able to migrate from the monolithic decision file to the sharded layout using the plumb migrate command
The system must implement spec updates using a single LLM call per spec file with an output schema containing section_updates and new_sections
The system must make a second call only when new_sections is non-empty
Structural reasoning for section placement must be kept separate from content generation to maintain clarity
The system must keep edit operations simple without complex operation sets
The system must accept the risk of unintended edits in exchange for performance gains while mitigating through clear prompting
The legacy decisions path function must be maintained for migration detection purposes
The primary system must operate on branch-specific decision logs
The hook must analyze the staged diff and read Claude Code's native session files directly from auto-detected paths
The system must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics
Within-batch similarity deduplication must prevent duplicate decisions in the same batch
Time awareness must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs, taking the later of the two
The system must pass current decisions and recent decisions from the last couple commits to the deduplication pass using a context window of 200 decisions
The system must use exact deduplication and LLM-based semantic deduplication with groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b configured for both decision_deduplicator and question_synthesizer (8192 max_tokens)
Source summaries must be structured as per-file mappings to enable granular tracking
All documentation files including SKILL files and CLAUDE.md must be kept consistent with the same sync workflow requirements
The plumb init command must check that the current directory is a git repository and exit with an error if not
The plumb init command must create the .plumb/ directory if it does not exist
The plumb init command must prompt the user interactively to provide a path to a spec file or directory of spec markdown files
The plumb init command must validate that the spec path exists and contains .md files
The plumb init command must use recursive search (rglob) to find markdown files within directories and suggest discovered spec files
The plumb init command must prompt the user to provide a path to a test file or test directory
The plumb init command must validate that the test path exists
The plumb init command must scan repository for test directories and files to provide suggestions
The plumb init command must write .plumb/config.json with the provided paths
The plumb init command must create a .plumbignore file in the project root if it does not exist
The plumb init command must install the git pre-commit hook by writing a script to .git/hooks/pre-commit that calls plumb hook
The plumb init command must set the pre-commit hook script as executable
The plumb init command must install the Claude Code skill locally by copying plumb/skill/SKILL.md to .claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md in the project root
The plumb init command must create .claude/skills/plumb/ directories if they do not exist
The plumb init command must only perform project-local installation and never write to the user's global ~/.claude/ directory
The plumb init command must append a Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md at the project root
The plumb init command must create CLAUDE.md if it does not exist
The plumb init command must run plumb parse-spec to do an initial parse of the spec into requirements
The plumb init command must print a confirmation summary to the terminal, including confirmation that the skill was installed at .claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md
The plumb hook command must read .plumb/config.json and exit 0 silently if not found
The plumb hook command must get the current staged diff via git diff --cached
The plumb hook command must get the current branch name
The plumb hook command must detect amends by comparing the HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit
If amend is detected, the plumb hook command must delete decisions in decisions.jsonl where commit_sha == last_commit before re-running analysis
The plumb hook command must detect broken references by checking all SHAs in decisions.jsonl against git history
The plumb hook command must flag unreachable SHAs with ref_status: broken and include a warning in output
The plumb hook command must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff
The plumb hook command must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log
If conversation log is found, the plumb hook command must read and chunk turns since last_commit timestamp
The plumb hook command must use unified conversation reading interface to support multiple sessions
The plumb hook command must merge conversation turns from all relevant session files modified after the last commit and sort chronologically
The plumb hook command must handle multi-line assistant responses that span multiple JSONL lines
The plumb hook command must convert tool_use blocks to '[tool: Name] description' format in conversation turns
The plumb hook command must run Decision Extraction per chunk
If conversation log is not found, the plumb hook command must skip conversation analysis and note conversation_available: false in each decision object
The plumb hook command must filter out decisions marked as non-spec-relevant during extraction before creating Decision records
The plumb hook command must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks using DuckDB for cross-shard queries
Deduplication must check against all existing decisions (both pending and resolved) instead of only resolved ones
The plumb hook command must implement within-batch similarity deduplication to compare new decisions against each other using Jaccard similarity check
For each decision with no associated question, the plumb hook command must run Question Synthesizer with program-specific model configuration
The plumb hook command must write all new decisions with status: pending to decisions.jsonl
The plumb hook command must implement decisions sharding using a TDD approach with core refactor, DuckDB integration, caller updates, new commands, and comprehensive testing coverage
The plumb hook command must run plumb parse-spec to update requirements cache for any modified spec files
The plumb hook command must batch spec updates across all decisions instead of running spec update for each individual decision
The plumb hook command must use search-and-replace pairs pattern for spec updates, where LLM returns list of {old_text, new_text} operations
The plumb hook command must implement a two-tier approach for spec updates: Tier 1 for existing section updates and Tier 2 for structural changes
For structural changes, the plumb hook command must use outline-first approach where LLM returns updated header outline first, then fills in content for new sections
The plumb hook command must create OutlineMerger signature for conditional second call that takes current outline and new headers, returns merged outline with proper positioning
If pending decisions exist, the plumb hook command must check whether it is running in a TTY or as a subprocess
In TTY mode, the plumb hook command must print a human-readable summary of pending decisions with instructions to run plumb review
In non-TTY mode, the plumb hook command must print a machine-readable JSON object to stdout
The plumb hook command must exit non-zero in both cases when pending decisions exist, aborting the commit
If no pending decisions exist, the plumb hook command must run plumb coverage, print a brief summary, update last_commit and last_commit_branch in config.json, and exit 0
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
If Plumb itself fails, it must print a warning to stderr and exit 0 so the commit is not blocked
The plumb hook --dry-run command must run the full hook analysis on staged changes but not write to decisions.jsonl and always exit 0
The plumb diff command must read staged changes via git diff --cached
The plumb diff command must run Diff Analysis on the staged diff
The plumb diff command must read and chunk the conversation log (turns since last_commit) if available
The plumb diff command must run Decision Extraction per chunk
The plumb diff command must print a preview to the terminal including summary of staged code changes, estimated number of decisions, and brief description of each estimated decision
The plumb diff command must make no writes to .plumb/
The plumb review command must read .plumb/decisions.jsonl and filter for status == pending
The plumb review command must accept an optional --branch <name> flag to filter by branch
When multiple session files exist, the plumb review command must use the most recent session file by modification time or match the current session ID for session selection
If no pending decisions exist, the plumb review command must print 'No pending decisions.' and exit 0
For each pending decision, the plumb review command must display the framing question, the decision made, the branch, file and line references, ref_status, and the most related current spec text
The plumb review command must prompt the user with options: [a]pprove, [r]eject, [e]dit, [s]kip
For reject option, the plumb review command must prompt for reason and optionally run plumb modify <id> automatically
For edit option, the plumb review command must allow user to provide replacement decision text
After all decisions are resolved, the plumb review command must run plumb sync for all approved/edited decisions
Decision classification must default spec_relevant field to True to ensure decisions pass through for user review when LLM classification is uncertain
The plumb approve <id> command must approve a single decision by ID and update its status to approved in decisions.jsonl
The plumb approve <id> command must then run plumb sync for that decision only
The status display must include a visual bar chart representation for coverage statistics
The status display must use Rich's Status spinner that updates in-place for progress indication
The plumb reject <id> command must reject a single decision by ID and update its status to rejected
The plumb reject <id> command must record the rejection reason
The plumb reject <id> command must not modify code or spec
The plumb edit <id> command must replace the decision text for a given decision ID with user-provided text
The plumb edit <id> command must update status to edited
The plumb edit <id> command must then run plumb sync for that decision only
The plumb modify <id> command must read the decision object for <id> from decisions.jsonl and verify status == rejected
The plumb modify <id> command must read the staged diff
The plumb modify <id> command must call the Claude API with the staged diff, decision, rejection reason, current spec, and modification instruction
The plumb modify <id> command must apply the proposed modification to the staged files
The plumb modify <id> command must run pytest on the test suite to verify tests still pass after modification
If tests pass, the plumb modify <id> command must print the resulting diff, stage the modified files, and update decision status to rejected_modified
If tests fail, the plumb modify <id> command must print the failure output, not stage the modification, prompt user to resolve manually, and update decision status to rejected_manual
In non-TTY mode, the plumb modify <id> command must return a machine-readable JSON result
plumb must never commit the modification, only stage it
The plumb sync command must add early exit check to avoid expensive operations when no unsynced decisions exist
The plumb sync command must read decisions from decisions.jsonl with status approved or edited that have not yet been synced
For each spec file, the plumb sync command must run WholeFileSpecUpdater to process all decisions affecting the spec file together
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must return section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
If new_sections is non-empty, the plumb sync command must run OutlineMerger to determine proper positioning of new sections
The plumb sync command must write the updated spec file to disk using temp file → rename
The plumb sync command must fix newline formatting by ensuring proper newlines between section headers and body content
The plumb sync command must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for requirements not covered by existing tests
Tests must be organized by functionality with proper requirement links in the format test_<req_id>_<description>
The generator must read only source files referenced by file_refs on synced decisions for context
The plumb sync command must write generated tests to test_generated.py using temp file → rename
The plumb sync command must run plumb parse-spec to re-cache requirements
The plumb sync command must set synced_at on each processed decision
The plumb sync command must print a summary of spec sections updated and test files created
The plumb sync command must use .plumbignore file with default patterns for documentation, build files, and IDE configurations
The plumb parse-spec command must read all markdown files in spec_paths from config.json
For each file or paragraph block, the plumb parse-spec command must run Requirement Parser to produce explicit, testable requirement statements
The plumb parse-spec command must assign each requirement a stable ID based on a hash of its content
The plumb parse-spec command must write results to .plumb/requirements.json
Requirements with matching hashes must not be re-processed, preserving existing metadata including created_at and last_seen_commit timestamps
The plumb parse-spec command must use regex patterns PLUMB_MARKER_RE and FUNC_NAME_RE to extract requirement IDs from test files for coverage mapping
The plumb parse-spec command must process all requirements and AST summaries of every non-test .py file through the CodeCoverageMapper using chunked mapping approach
The plumb parse-spec command must use fan-out chunking to group source files under token budget (default 60000 tokens) and broadcast requirements to each chunk
The plumb parse-spec command must implement granular per-file hashing with structured v2 cache format that tracks individual requirement results for efficient cache invalidation
The plumb parse-spec command must preserve existing incremental caching logic by applying chunking inside the LLM call after dirty requirements are determined
The plumb parse-spec command must extract source file paths from evidence strings using pattern matching against known files
The plumb parse-spec command must use greedy packing to group source/test summaries into chunks and ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent chunk processing
The plumb parse-spec command must merge results from multiple chunks using custom logic that ORs 'implemented' flags and concatenates evidence strings per requirement_id
The plumb coverage command must run pytest --cov and parse output for code coverage
The plumb coverage command must report line coverage percentage
For spec-to-test coverage, the plumb coverage command must check whether a test references or maps to each requirement using string-matching to look for requirement IDs literally in test files
The plumb coverage command must scan for link markers in test files to determine coverage
The plumb coverage command must report count and percentage covered for spec-to-test coverage
For spec-to-code coverage, the plumb coverage command must use the requirements cache to check whether each requirement has a corresponding implementation
The plumb coverage command must use string-matching to look for requirement IDs literally in source files
The plumb coverage command must report gaps in spec-to-code coverage
The plumb coverage command must print a formatted table using rich
The plumb coverage command must use LLM calls to refresh the requirements cache for accurate coverage analysis
The plumb coverage command must measure and report coverage improvement achieved by marker injection
The plumb status command must print tracked spec files and total requirements
The plumb status command must print number of tests
The plumb status command must print pending decisions, with branch breakdown if spanning multiple branches
The plumb status command must print decisions with broken git references
The plumb status command must print last sync commit
The plumb status command must print coverage summary for all three dimensions
The skill file must be located at plumb/skill/SKILL.md in the package
The skill file must be installed to <project_root>/.claude/SKILL.md per project
The skill installation must be local to the project, not installed globally, and not shared across projects
The .claude/ directory and SKILL.md must be committed to version control so all contributors get the skill automatically
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing from the ~/.claude/projects/ directory structure
Conversation logs must be located in the real session JSONL files using the format ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-uuid>.jsonl
Plumb must attempt to auto-detect Claude Code log locations if claude_log_path is not set in config.json
If conversation log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis, note conversation_available: false in each decision, print a warning, and continue with diff-only analysis
The log must be a JSONL file where each line is a turn with at minimum role, content, and timestamp
Session files must be pre-filtered by modification time to avoid reading old files
Individual entries must be filtered by timestamp
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the last_commit timestamp in config.json
The conversation parser must handle the native session JSONL format with type/message schema used by Claude Code
Chunking must be performed in conversation.py before any DSPy program is called
No LLM must be involved in the chunking step
Chunks must be created deterministically
The primary unit for chunking must be user turn
A chunk must be one user message plus all following assistant turns up to but not including the next user message
If a chunk exceeds 6,000 tokens, it must be split at tool call boundaries
If no tool call boundary exists, chunks must be split at the midpoint of the largest assistant turn
The final assistant turn of the previous chunk must be prepended as a header to the next chunk for overlap
One turn of overlap must preserve continuity without meaningfully increasing size
Before chunking, tool result turns longer than 500 tokens whose content appears to be a raw file read must be replaced with [file read: <filename>]
Assistant messages that span multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled
DecisionExtractor must be called once per chunk with the diff_summary passed identically to every call
Results must be merged after all chunks with near-duplicate decisions collapsed into one, preserving the earliest chunk_index
The pre-commit hook must fire on amends
To prevent duplicates on amends, the system must compare HEAD's parent SHA to last_commit
If SHAs are equal, the system must delete decisions where commit_sha == last_commit before re-running analysis
On every hook run, the system must check all stored SHAs against git history
Unreachable SHAs must be flagged ref_status: broken
Plumb must not attempt to re-map decisions to new SHAs after a rebase
The user must review and re-resolve broken-reference decisions manually
The decision log must be append-only
Existing lines in the decision log must never be modified in place
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id
The latest line for a given id must be canonical
commit_sha must be null until the commit lands
commit_sha must be populated by the hook on the second pass when no pending decisions remain and the commit proceeds
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input
DiffAnalyzer must output list of change summaries with files_changed, summary, and change_type
DiffAnalyzer must group related changes into logical units
DiffAnalyzer must not invent meaning
DecisionExtractor must take chunk and diff_summary as input
DecisionExtractor must output list of decision objects with question, decision, made_by, related_diff_summary, and confidence
DecisionExtractor must extract explicit and implicit decisions
DecisionExtractor must not extract trivial decisions like variable naming or import ordering
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no associated question as input
QuestionSynthesizer must output a plain-English question framing the decision for a developer
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input
RequirementParser must output list of requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields
RequirementParser must use atomic statements, active voice, no duplicates
RequirementParser must flag vague statements as ambiguous: true and exclude unless user approves
SpecUpdater must take spec_section (markdown) and decision (approved decision object) as input
SpecUpdater must output updated markdown for that section
SpecUpdater must capture result of decision as natural requirement
SpecUpdater must not reference the decision itself
SpecUpdater must preserve existing formatting
TestGenerator must take requirements (uncovered), existing_tests (file contents), and code_context (relevant source) as input
TestGenerator must output runnable pytest test code as a Python string
TestGenerator must create one function per requirement with descriptive names (test_<req_id>_<description>)
TestGenerator must create tests that contain real assertions against actual code under test
TestGenerator must not use pytest.skip() or # TODO: implement
TestGenerator must not overwrite existing tests
CodeModifier must be used by plumb modify and called via the Anthropic API directly with a structured prompt
CodeModifier must take staged diff, rejected decision, rejection reason, and current spec as input
CodeModifier must output modified file contents that satisfy the rejection while remaining consistent with the spec
Plumb must apply the CodeModifier output, run pytest, and stage the result only if tests pass
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with a clear error message if config.json is missing or malformed
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure (max 2 retries) then raise PlumbInferenceError with a human-readable message
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
Hook failures must print a warning to stderr and exit 0
File writes (spec updates, test generation) must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes
If conversation log is unavailable, Plumb must continue with diff-only analysis and this is not an error
If plumb modify test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and update decision status to rejected_manual
The plumb status command must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls
The plumb coverage command must display progress using an in-place updating spinner, then print the final results table
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times to skip files that cannot contain relevant conversations
Plumb itself must have pytest testing with 80% coverage minimum for v0.1.0
cli.py must be tested so all commands run without error given valid inputs
cli.py tests must verify per-decision commands update decisions.jsonl correctly
cli.py tests must verify coverage command provides progress feedback with progress bar and status text
cli.py tests must verify spec and test suggestion functions cover various directory scenarios
decision_log.py must be tested for read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl
decision_log.py tests must verify latest-line-wins logic for status updates
git_hook.py must be tested so hook produces correct pending decisions given mock diffs and conversation logs
git_hook.py tests must verify amend detection
git_hook.py tests must verify TTY vs non-TTY output formats
conversation.py must be tested for correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, and metadata
conversation.py tests must verify oversized chunks split at tool call boundaries
programs/ must be tested so each DSPy program produces correctly structured output given fixture inputs
programs/ tests must verify schema validity, not LLM quality
programs/ must have comprehensive test suite for chunking functionality covering token estimation, item chunking, and chunked mapper execution scenarios
coverage_reporter.py must be tested for correct calculations given mock pytest output
sync.py must be tested so spec and test files are updated correctly given approved decisions
sync.py tests must verify no partial writes
sync.py must have newline formatting tests covering both apply_section_updates() and insert_new_sections() functions
Plumb supports loading environment variables from .env files to manage configuration settings
The plumb init command creates a .env file in the project directory to facilitate environment-based configuration management
The system supports ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files
Tests are linked to requirements through requirement ID comments (e.g., # plumb:req-XXXXXXXX) to establish traceability between test functions and specific requirements
All tests are organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component
Tests without requirement links are treated as sync violations that indicate either missing requirements or unnecessary tests
The system supports file ignore patterns through a '.plumbignore' file
The system provides a '--all' command option for the 'plumb approve' command to approve all changes at once
Generated cache and coverage files are excluded from commits
The system includes a 'check' command as an alias for manual decision scanning
Plumb can handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically to support exit-and-relaunch scenarios
The sync command provides progress indicators with status updates to give users feedback during operation
The workflow requires an explicit sync step after approving decisions, with staging of sync output before re-committing changes
The system uses a branch-sharded structure for decision logs
The system provides CLI commands for merging decision logs from different branches
Users can migrate existing decision logs from the old monolithic format to the new branch-sharded layout through dedicated CLI commands
The plumb merge-decisions <branch> command allows users to merge decisions from feature branches back to main
A find_decision_branch() function helps locate which branch file contains a specific decision ID

The core LLM integration is designed with a WholeFileSpecUpdater that takes full spec content and decisions as input, and outputs section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
This approach accepts rewriting whole sections instead of making surgical edits for markdown specs
An OutlineMerger component handles structural changes when new sections need to be added or content needs reordering

The system includes helper functions for DuckDB result type conversion, using _clean_duckdb_row() and _to_python_native() functions to convert DuckDB result types to Python native types for Pydantic compatibility
Comprehensive test coverage is implemented for migration and merge functionality, including edge cases and error conditions
Plumb solves a bounded problem and should be holdable in a single programmer's head
All LLM-powered functions are implemented as DSPy programs, not open-ended agents
DSPy programs ensure they are controllable, auditable, and reliable
Plumb uses the Anthropic Claude SDK (via the user's existing account) as its inference provider
Claude Sonnet 4.6 serves as the default model for all programs
Plumb operates as a git hook and CLI tool
Plumb does not change how the user writes code
The pre-commit hook ensures that every committed state has been reviewed and approved
A commit represents a fully reconciled snapshot of spec, tests, and code
Plumb uses Claude Code session data when available
When Claude Code session data is not available, Plumb falls back to diff-only analysis
Decisions still get captured when falling back to diff-only analysis; they are just derived from code changes rather than reasoning
The system prevents duplicate decisions through a multi-stage pipeline using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis to catch different types of duplicates
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models are captured as decisions
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings are filtered out
Spec updates use a search-and-replace approach with edits containing old_text/new_text pairs rather than full section replacement for more precise modifications
Section updates use exact header matching first, then fall back to normalized matching (stripped whitespace, case-insensitive) to handle minor variations in header formatting
Plumb stores all state in a .plumb/ folder at the root of the user's repository
The .plumb/ folder should be committed to version control
Plumb intercepts commits via a git pre-commit hook
The commit is the gate, and nothing is committed until decisions have been reviewed
The conversation log reader must work seamlessly across different repositories without requiring additional user setup or configuration overhead
plumb init appends a Plumb status block to CLAUDE.md at the project root
The Plumb status block is delimited by comment markers so future Plumb commands can update it without affecting surrounding content
When parsing session files, assistant entries that contain 'thinking' blocks, have 'isSidechain: true', or have 'isMeta: true' must be skipped
